normalized_title: Canning Bridge Activity Centre Plan (City of Melville and City of South Perth)
version_date: Consolidated version incorporating Amendments 1-5; Amendment 5 (R-Codes Vol. 2 Parts 3-4 introduction and built form changes to Q3-Q5) endorsed by WAPC 22 September 2020
scope_summary: Statutory activity centre plan for the Canning Bridge area (recognised as an activity centre under SPP 4.2), spanning land in both the City of Melville and the City of South Perth around the Canning Bridge bus/rail interchange at the Canning Highway and Kwinana Freeway junction. Part One (Statutory Section) sets land use permissibility, built form requirements (building heights by zone M15/M10/H8/H4, street setbacks, podium controls), dwelling diversity, parking ratios and design guidelines across Quarters Q1-Q6, operating alongside the local planning schemes; Part Two is explanatory. Where the CBACP provides a more specific design outcome than the R-Codes, the CBACP prevails.

key_numeric_standards.value: 15 storeys, not exceeding 48 m above natural ground level
10 storeys, not exceeding 32 m above natural ground level
8 storeys, not exceeding 26 m above natural ground level
4 storeys, not exceeding 16 m above natural ground level

key_numeric_standards.topic: H4 zone height concession in Q3/Q4/Q5
key_numeric_standards.value: up to 6 storeys (20 m above NGL) where site area is at least 1,200 m2
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: H4-zoned lots in Quarters Q3, Q4 and Q5

key_numeric_standards.value: 20 m within 5 m of the property boundary
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: development adjoining Residential-zoned sites / residential streets

key_numeric_standards.topic: street setback - H4 zones in Q3/Q4/Q5
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: all development within H4 zones in Quarters Q3, Q4 and Q5

key_numeric_standards.value: minimum 20% and maximum 50% one-bedroom/studio dwellings; minimum 40% two-bedroom dwellings

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: developments greater than 12 dwellings in Q3, Q4 and Q5

key_numeric_standards.value: not less than 1 bay per 50 m2 nla and not more than 1 bay per 25 m2 nla (Q1/Q2); not less than 1 bay per 50 m2 nla (Q3-Q5)
1 bay per dwelling; plus visitor bicycle parking of 1 bay per 10 dwellings in Q3-Q5
1 space per 10 car bays (residential, developments over 20 dwellings in Q3-Q5); 1 bay per 5 required car bays (non-residential)

definitions: Building Height (in metres and in storeys)

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 4.2: Activity Centres for Perth and Peel
State Planning Policy 7.3 - Residential Design Codes (R-Codes)
Building Code of Australia / National Construction Code
City of Melville Community Planning Scheme No. 5 (CPS5)
City of South Perth Town Planning Scheme No. 6 (TPS6)

quality_flags: Rotated section-tab/sidebar text extracted reversed throughout (e.g. 'yrammuS evitucetxE', 'senilediuG ngiseD') - noise only, body text unaffected
Multi-column page layout interleaved in extraction: explanatory sidebar text is merged into statutory clause lines, so some clauses need careful de-interleaving to read
Vertical map labels extracted one character per line in places
Tables (version control, parking ratios cl 18.3) flattened to plain text with column values run together
Scheme references (CPS5/TPS6) predate Melville LPS6; the plan body refers to the older schemes operative when it was prepared
